reasoning: Lee Nak-yon is a former Prime Minister who left the main opposition Democratic Party (DP) to form a new minor party, New Future Democratic Party. Polling around March showed him with less than 2% support. Furthermore, on April 28, 2025, the DP officially nominated Lee Jae-myung as its presidential candidate. In South Korea's highly polarized two-party system, a minor party candidate polling around 1-2% has virtually no chance of winning the snap election, which must occur by June 3, 2025. Given the market price on Polymarket was 0.00 in early May, and there is no evidence of any sudden surge or viability for Lee Nak-yon, the probability of him winning the election is extremely close to 0%. I submit 2% to account for unpredictable tail events (e.g., massive unprecedented scandal wiping out both major party candidates simultaneously within a 3-week window, somehow causing him to be the compromise candidate).
